ayahs.translation: And recite to them the news of Noah, when he said to his people, "O my people, if my residence and my reminding of the signs of Allāh has become burdensome upon you—then I have relied upon Allāh. So resolve upon your plan and your associates. Then let not your plan be obscure to you. Then carry it out upon me and do not give me respite."
ayahs.summary: Noah told his people: if my stay and reminder burden you, I rely on Allah; do your worst.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Recite to them the story of Noah when he said to his people: O my people, if my presence and my reminding you of the signs of Allah are burdensome to you—I have put my trust in Allah. So resolve your plan and your partners, then let your plan not be obscure to you, then carry it out against me and give me no respite. Noah relies on Allah and challenges them to do their worst.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Noah's trust in Allah and his fearless challenge to his people are a model for every caller to the truth.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The punishment of those who invent falsehood was stated; now the story of Noah begins.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: If you turn away, I asked you no reward; my reward is only from Allah and I am commanded to be among the Muslims.

ayahs.arabic: فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَمَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ ٧٢
ayahs.translation: And if you turn away—then no payment have I asked of you. My reward is only from Allāh, and I have been commanded to be of the Muslims."
ayahs.summary: If you turn away, I asked no wage; my reward is from Allah; I am commanded to submit.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: If you turn away, I asked you no reward; my reward is only from Allah, and I have been commanded to be among the Muslims. Noah seeks no wage from them; his reward is with Allah and his duty is to submit.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The messenger seeks no worldly reward; his reward is from Allah and his command is to submit.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Noah challenged his people and put his trust in Allah; now he declares he seeks no reward from them.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: They denied him; We saved him and those with him in the ship and made them successors; We drowned those who denied Our signs.

ayahs.arabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ وَأَغْرَقْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ ٧٣
ayahs.translation: And they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the ship and made them successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. Then see how was the end of those who were warned.
ayahs.summary: They denied him; We saved him and his companions in the ship and drowned the deniers.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: They denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the ship and made them successors (khalāʾif), and We drowned those who denied Our signs. So see how was the end of those who were warned. Salvation for the believers, destruction for the deniers—the pattern is clear.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Noah and the believers were saved; the deniers were drowned; the end of those who were warned is a lesson for all.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Noah said his reward is from Allah; now the denial, the salvation in the ship, and the drowning of the deniers are related.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Then We sent after him messengers to their peoples with clear proofs; they did not believe in what they had denied before.

ayahs.arabic: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ رُسُلًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِمْ فَجَآءُوهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَمَا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِمَا كَذَّبُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ ٧٤
ayahs.translation: Then We sent after him messengers to their peoples, and they came to them with clear proofs. But they were not to believe in that which they had denied before. Thus We seal over the hearts of the transgressors.
ayahs.summary: Later messengers came with clear proofs but they did not believe; thus hearts of transgressors are sealed.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Then We sent after him messengers to their peoples; they came with clear proofs but they would not believe in what they had denied before. Thus We seal the hearts of the transgressors. Persistence in denial after clear proofs leads to the sealing of the heart.
ayahs.lessonReflection: To deny again after clear proofs is to risk the sealing of the heart—the same pattern repeats.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Noah's people were drowned and the end of the warned was shown; now the messengers after him and the sealing of hearts are stated.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Then We sent Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his chiefs with Our signs; they were arrogant and were a sinful people.

ayahs.arabic: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ قَوْمًۭا مُّجْرِمِينَ ٧٥
ayahs.translation: Then We sent after them Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his establishment with Our signs, but they behaved arrogantly and were a criminal people.
ayahs.summary: Then We sent Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh with Our signs; they were arrogant and criminal.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Then We sent after them Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his chiefs with Our signs, but they were arrogant (istakbarū) and were a sinful people. Arrogance and sin combined when they faced the clear signs.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Moses and Aaron came with signs; Pharaoh and his people responded with arrogance and sin.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The messengers after Noah and the sealing of hearts were described; now Moses and Aaron are sent to Pharaoh.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: When the truth came to them from Us they said: this is clear magic.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِنْ عِندِنَا قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسِحْرٌۭ مُّبِينٌۭ ٧٦
ayahs.translation: So when there came to them the truth from Us, they said, "Indeed, this is obvious magic."
ayahs.summary: When the truth came from Us they said it was clear magic.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: When the truth came to them from Us they said: this is clear magic. The same response as with the Qur'an—dismissing the truth as magic when it does not suit them.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Calling the truth magic is the repeated response of those who refuse to submit.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Pharaoh and his people were arrogant and sinful; when the truth came they called it magic.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Moses said: do you say this about the truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? The magicians do not succeed.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ مُوسَىٰٓ أَتَقُولُونَ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَكُمْ ۖ أَسِحْرٌ هَـٰذَا وَلَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلسَّـٰحِرُونَ ٧٧
ayahs.translation: Moses said, "Do you say thus about the truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? But magicians will not succeed."
ayahs.summary: Moses said: Do you call the truth magic? Magicians do not succeed.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Moses said: do you say this about the truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? The magicians will not succeed. Moses turns their accusation back: the truth is truth; magic is false and magicians do not succeed.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The truth is not magic; to call it so is to confuse the two—and magicians do not succeed.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They called the truth magic; Moses responds that the truth is truth and magicians do not succeed.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: They said: have you come to turn us from what we found our fathers upon, and that greatness might be yours in the land?

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ أَجِئْتَنَا لِتَلْفِتَنَا عَمَّا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ ءَابَآءَنَا وَتَكُونَ لَكُمَا ٱلْكِبْرِيَآءُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا نَحْنُ لَكُمَا بِمُؤْمِنِينَ ٧٨
ayahs.translation: They said, "Have you come to us to turn us away from that upon which we found our fathers and so that you two may have grandeur in the land? And we are not believers in you."
ayahs.summary: They said: Did you come to turn us from our fathers' way and seek grandeur? We do not believe you.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: They said: have you come to us to turn us away from what we found our fathers upon, and that the two of you might have greatness in the land? We do not believe you. The argument of following the fathers and the charge of seeking power—both are rejected.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Attachment to the ways of the fathers and the accusation of seeking power are the classic excuses of the rejecters.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Moses defended the truth; they respond with the argument of the fathers and denial.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Pharaoh said: bring me every skilled magician.

ayahs.arabic: وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ٱئْتُونِى بِكُلِّ سَـٰحِرٍ عَلِيمٍۢ ٧٩
ayahs.translation: And Pharaoh said, "Bring to me every learned magician."
ayahs.summary: Pharaoh demanded every skilled magician.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Pharaoh said: bring me every skilled magician. He seeks to match the signs with magic—the contest between Moses and the magicians follows in the full story elsewhere; here the focus is on the rejection and its outcome.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Pharaoh's response is to summon magicians—to oppose the truth with illusion.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They accused Moses of seeking to turn them from their fathers' way; Pharaoh demands magicians.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: When the magicians came, Moses said: cast what you will cast.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ ٨٠
ayahs.translation: So when the magicians came, Moses said to them, "Throw down whatever you will throw."
ayahs.summary: When the magicians came, Moses told them to throw what they would.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: When the magicians came Moses said to them: cast what you are going to cast. The challenge is direct; the outcome—the magicians' staffs and ropes appearing to swallow, then Moses' staff swallowing what they fabricated—is known from other surahs. Here the theme is the confrontation.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The messenger confronts the illusion with the truth; the truth prevails.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Pharaoh brought the magicians; Moses invites them to cast what they will.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: When Moses had cast, he said: what you have brought is magic; Allah will bring it to naught.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّآ أَلْقَوْا۟ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ مَا جِئْتُم بِهِ ٱلسِّحْرُ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَيُبْطِلُهُۥٓ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُصْلِحُ عَمَلَ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ ٨١
ayahs.translation: And when they had thrown, Moses said, "What you have brought is only magic. Indeed, Allāh will expose its worthlessness. Indeed, Allāh does not amend the work of corrupters.
ayahs.summary: Moses said their display was magic; Allah will invalidate it and does not approve corrupters' work.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: When Moses had cast, he said: what you have brought is magic; Allah will invalidate it; Allah does not set right the work of the corrupters. The magicians' act is declared magic and Allah will nullify it; He does not support the work of those who spread corruption.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Allah invalidates the false and does not set right the work of the corrupters.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The magicians cast; Moses declares it magic and that Allah will invalidate it.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Allah confirms the truth by His words though the guilty dislike it.

ayahs.arabic: وَيُحِقُّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ ٨٢
ayahs.translation: And Allāh will establish the truth by His words, even if the criminals dislike it."
ayahs.summary: Allah confirms the truth by His words though the guilty dislike it.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Allah confirms the truth by His words though the guilty dislike it. The truth is established by Allah's word regardless of the rejecters' displeasure.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The truth is established by Allah; the displeasure of the guilty does not change it.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Allah was said to invalidate the magic and not to set right the work of the corrupters; now He confirms the truth by His words.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: None believed in Moses except offspring of his people, for fear of Pharaoh and his chiefs.

ayahs.arabic: فَمَآ ءَامَنَ لِمُوسَىٰٓ إِلَّا ذُرِّيَّةٌۭ مِّن قَوْمِهِۦ عَلَىٰ خَوْفٍۢ مِّن فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِمْ أَن يَفْتِنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ لَعَالٍۢ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ ٨٣
ayahs.translation: But no one believed Moses, except some offspring among his people, for fear of Pharaoh and his establishment that they would persecute them. And indeed, Pharaoh was haughty within the land, and indeed, he was of the transgressors.
ayahs.summary: Only some youth of his people believed Moses, fearing Pharaoh, who was arrogant and oppressive.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: None believed in Moses except a seed of his people, for fear of Pharaoh and his chiefs, lest he persecute them. Pharaoh was indeed mighty on earth and one of the wanton. Fear of the tyrant kept many from declaring belief; only a few had the courage.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Belief under tyranny requires courage; many stayed silent for fear of Pharaoh.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Allah confirms the truth by His words; now the few who believed in Moses despite fear of Pharaoh are mentioned.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Moses said: O my people, if you believe in Allah then put your trust in Him, if you are Muslims.

ayahs.arabic: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِن كُنتُمْ ءَامَنتُم بِٱللَّهِ فَعَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلُوٓا۟ إِن كُنتُم مُّسْلِمِينَ ٨٤
ayahs.translation: And Moses said, "O my people, if you have believed in Allāh, then rely upon Him, if you should be Muslims."
ayahs.summary: Moses said: If you believe in Allah, rely on Him if you are submitters.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Moses said: O my people, if you have believed in Allah then put your trust in Him, if you are Muslims. They said: in Allah we put our trust. Our Lord, do not make us a trial for the wrongdoing people, and save us by Your mercy from the disbelieving people. The believers put their trust in Allah and ask not to be made a trial and to be saved from the wrongdoers.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Trust in Allah and the prayer not to be made a trial are the response of the believing community under oppression.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Only a few believed for fear of Pharaoh; Moses calls them to put their trust in Allah.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We revealed to Moses and his brother: appoint for your people houses in Egypt and make your houses a direction for prayer.

ayahs.arabic: فَقَالُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةًۭ لِّلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ ٨٥
ayahs.translation: So they said, "Upon Allāh do we rely. Our Lord, make us not trial for the wrongdoing people.
ayahs.summary: They said: We rely on Allah; our Lord, do not make us a trial for the wrongdoers.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: We revealed to Moses and his brother: provide houses for your people in Egypt and make your houses a qibla and establish prayer. Give good tidings to the believers. The believers are given a place and a direction—establishing worship and community under persecution.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Houses as places of safety and prayer and the establishment of worship are the response to oppression.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Moses and the believers put their trust in Allah and prayed for deliverance; now they are told to establish houses and prayer.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Moses said: our Lord, You have given Pharaoh and his chiefs adornment and wealth in the life of this world.

ayahs.arabic: وَنَجِّنَا بِرَحْمَتِكَ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ ٨٦
ayahs.translation: And save us by Your mercy from the disbelieving people."
ayahs.summary: Save us by Your mercy from the disbelieving people.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Moses said: our Lord, You have given Pharaoh and his chiefs adornment and wealth in the life of this world—our Lord, that they may lead people astray from Your path. Our Lord, destroy their wealth and harden their hearts so they do not believe until they see the painful punishment. Moses prays against the oppressors' means of misguidance and for their hearts to be hardened until they see the punishment.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The prophet prays against the wealth and influence that lead people astray and for the exposure of the stubborn until they see the punishment.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Houses and prayer were commanded; Moses now prays against Pharaoh's adornment and for the exposure of the stubborn.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Allah said: your prayer is answered; so go straight and do not follow the path of those who do not know.

ayahs.arabic: وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ وَأَخِيهِ أَن تَبَوَّءَا لِقَوْمِكُمَا بِمِصْرَ بُيُوتًۭا وَٱجْعَلُوا۟ بُيُوتَكُمْ قِبْلَةًۭ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ٨٧
ayahs.translation: And We inspired to Moses and his brother, "Settle your people in Egypt in houses and make your houses facing the qiblah and establish prayer and give good tidings to the believers."
ayahs.summary: We inspired Moses and his brother: settle your people in Egypt, make houses qiblah-oriented, establish prayer.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Allah said: your supplication is answered; so go straight and do not follow the path of those who do not know. The prayer is accepted; the command is to remain on the straight path and not imitate the ignorant.
ayahs.lessonReflection: When the prayer is answered, the response is to hold to the straight path and avoid the way of the ignorant.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Moses prayed against Pharaoh's means of misguidance; Allah answers and commands steadfastness.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We brought the Children of Israel across the sea; Pharaoh and his hosts pursued them in rebellion and hostility.

ayahs.arabic: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ ءَاتَيْتَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَأَهُۥ زِينَةًۭ وَأَمْوَٰلًۭا فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا رَبَّنَا لِيُضِلُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِكَ ۖ رَبَّنَا ٱطْمِسْ عَلَىٰٓ أَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَٱشْدُدْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَلَا يُؤْمِنُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ ٨٨
ayahs.translation: And Moses said, "Our Lord, indeed You have given Pharaoh and his establishment splendor and wealth in the worldly life, our Lord, that they may lead men astray from Your way. Our Lord, obliterate their wealth and harden their hearts so that they will not believe until they see the painful punishment."
ayahs.summary: Moses prayed: You gave Pharaoh splendor; obliterate their wealth and harden their hearts.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: We brought the Children of Israel across the sea; Pharaoh and his hosts pursued them in rebellion (baghy) and enmity until, when drowning overtook him, he said: I believe that there is no god but He in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am among the Muslims. Now? When he saw the punishment he claimed belief—but it was too late.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Pharaoh's belief at the moment of drowning was not accepted; belief under compulsion at the end is not the same as belief when the choice was open.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Allah answered Moses and told him to go straight; now the crossing of the sea and Pharaoh's pursuit and drowning are related.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Now? And you had disobeyed before and were among the corrupters.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ قَدْ أُجِيبَت دَّعْوَتُكُمَا فَٱسْتَقِيمَا وَلَا تَتَّبِعَآنِّ سَبِيلَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ ٨٩
ayahs.translation: Allāh said, "Your supplication has been answered. So remain on a right course and follow not the way of those who do not know."
ayahs.summary: Allah said: Your supplication is answered; remain steadfast and do not follow the ignorant.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Now? And you had disobeyed before and were among the corrupters. So today We save you in your body that you may be a sign to those after you—and indeed many among the people are heedless of Our signs. Pharaoh's body is saved as a sign; his late profession is rejected.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Belief at the last moment after a life of rebellion is rejected; his body is preserved as a sign for later generations.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Pharaoh claimed belief when drowning; Allah rejects it—he had disobeyed and been among the corrupters.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We settled the Children of Israel in a good settlement and provided them good things; they did not differ until knowledge came to them.

ayahs.arabic: ۞ وَجَـٰوَزْنَا بِبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱلْبَحْرَ فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ وَجُنُودُهُۥ بَغْيًۭا وَعَدْوًا ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَدْرَكَهُ ٱلْغَرَقُ قَالَ ءَامَنتُ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِىٓ ءَامَنَتْ بِهِۦ بَنُوٓا۟ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَأَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ ٩٠
ayahs.translation: And We took the Children of Israel across the sea, and Pharaoh and his soldiers pursued them in tyranny and enmity until, when drowning overtook him, he said, "I believe that there is no deity except that in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am of the Muslims."
ayahs.summary: We brought the Children of Israel across the sea; when drowning overtook Pharaoh he professed faith.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: We brought the Children of Israel across the sea. Pharaoh and his hosts pursued them in rebellion and enmity until, when the drowning overtook him, he said: I believe that there is no god but He in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am of the Muslims. The moment of desperation produces the words he had refused before.
ayahs.lessonReflection: When the sea closed over him, Pharaoh said what he had denied—but the acceptance of faith is with Allah, and the time had passed.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The crossing and pursuit were stated; now Pharaoh's words when drowning are given.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Allah says: now? When you had disobeyed before and were among the corrupters.

ayahs.arabic: ءَآلْـَٔـٰنَ وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ ٩١
ayahs.translation: Now? And you had disobeyed before and were of the corrupters?
ayahs.summary: Now you believe, after having disobeyed and spread corruption?
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Now? And you had disobeyed before and were among the corrupters. So today We save you in your body that you may be a sign to those after you—and indeed many of the people are heedless of Our signs. His body is preserved as a sign; his late profession does not save him.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The body is saved as a sign; the soul faces the consequence of a lifetime of rebellion.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Pharaoh said he believed when drowning; Allah responds: now? After your past disobedience.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We settled the Children of Israel in a good settlement and provided them good things.

ayahs.arabic: فَٱلْيَوْمَ نُنَجِّيكَ بِبَدَنِكَ لِتَكُونَ لِمَنْ خَلْفَكَ ءَايَةًۭ ۚ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًۭا مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا لَغَـٰفِلُونَ ٩٢
ayahs.translation: So today We will save you in body that you may be to those who succeed you a sign. And indeed, many among the people, of Our signs, are heedless.
ayahs.summary: We save your body as a sign for those after you; many are heedless of Our signs.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: So today We save you in your body that you may be a sign to those who come after you. And indeed many among the people are heedless of Our signs. Pharaoh's body is preserved—according to tradition, to be seen by later generations—as a sign; heedlessness of signs is widespread.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Pharaoh's body becomes a sign for later generations; many remain heedless of such signs.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Pharaoh's late profession was rejected; his body is saved as a sign.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We settled the Children of Israel in a good settlement and provided them good things; they did not differ until knowledge came.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَقَدْ بَوَّأْنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ مُبَوَّأَ صِدْقٍۢ وَرَزَقْنَـٰهُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ فَمَا ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْعِلْمُ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِى بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ٩٣
ayahs.translation: And We had certainly settled the Children of Israel in an agreeable settlement and provided them with good things. And they did not differ until knowledge had come to them. Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.
ayahs.summary: We settled the Children of Israel well and provided for them; your Lord will judge their differences on the Day of Resurrection.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: We settled the Children of Israel in a settlement of truth (mubawwaʾ ṣidq) and provided them with good things. They did not differ until knowledge came to them. Your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that in which they differed. They were given a good abode and provision; after knowledge came they differed; the judgment is deferred to the Day of Resurrection.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The Children of Israel were given a good settlement; their differences after knowledge came will be judged on the Day of Resurrection.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Pharaoh's body was saved as a sign; now the settlement of the Children of Israel and their subsequent differences are stated.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: If you are in doubt about what We have sent down to you, ask those who read the Scripture before you.

ayahs.arabic: فَإِن كُنتَ فِى شَكٍّۢ مِّمَّآ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ فَسْـَٔلِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَقْرَءُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلِكَ ۚ لَقَدْ جَآءَكَ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُمْتَرِينَ ٩٤
ayahs.translation: So if you are in doubt, about that which We have revealed to you, then ask those who have been reading the Scripture before you. The truth has certainly come to you from your Lord, so never be among the doubters.
ayahs.summary: If in doubt, ask the People of the Book; the truth has come from your Lord—do not doubt.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: If you are in doubt about what We have sent down to you, ask those who read the Scripture before you. The truth has come to you from your Lord, so do not be among the doubters. The Prophet is told that if he had any doubt—though he did not—he could ask the People of the Book; the truth is from his Lord and he must not doubt.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The truth has come from the Lord; doubt is removed by the evidence and by the testimony of the earlier Scriptures.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Children of Israel and their differences were mentioned; now the Prophet is told the truth has come and not to doubt.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: And do not be among those who deny the signs of Allah, lest you be among the losers.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ ٩٥
ayahs.translation: And never be of those who deny the signs of Allāh and thus be among the losers.
ayahs.summary: Do not be among those who deny Allah's signs and become losers.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: And do not be among those who deny the signs of Allah, so that you become among the losers. The warning is direct: denying the signs leads to loss. The Prophet and the believers are urged to hold fast to the signs.
ayahs.lessonReflection: To deny the signs of Allah is to choose loss; the believer holds fast to them.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet was told the truth has come and not to doubt; now he is warned not to deny the signs.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Those upon whom the word of your Lord has been fulfilled will not believe.

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِمْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ ٩٦
ayahs.translation: Indeed, those upon whom the word of your Lord has come into effect will not believe,
ayahs.summary: Those against whom your Lord's word is confirmed will not believe.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Those upon whom the word of your Lord has been fulfilled will not believe—even if every sign came to them—until they see the painful punishment. For some, the heart is sealed; they will not believe until they see the punishment, and then it is too late.
ayahs.lessonReflection: When the word of the Lord is fulfilled against someone, no sign will avail until they see the punishment.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet was warned not to deny the signs; now those upon whom the word has been fulfilled are described.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Why was there no town that believed and was helped by its belief except the people of Jonah?

ayahs.arabic: وَلَوْ جَآءَتْهُمْ كُلُّ ءَايَةٍ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ ٩٧
ayahs.translation: Even if every sign should come to them, until they see the painful punishment.
ayahs.summary: Not even if every sign came to them until they see the painful punishment.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Even if every sign came to them, they would not believe until they see the painful punishment. The sealing is so complete that only the sight of the punishment would affect them—and then belief would be of no avail.
ayahs.lessonReflection: For those upon whom the word is fulfilled, signs no longer open the heart; only the punishment will be seen.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Those upon whom the word was fulfilled were said not to believe; now the extent of their closure is stated.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: The people of Jonah believed and We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in the world and gave them enjoyment for a time.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَوْلَا كَانَتْ قَرْيَةٌ ءَامَنَتْ فَنَفَعَهَآ إِيمَـٰنُهَآ إِلَّا قَوْمَ يُونُسَ لَمَّآ ءَامَنُوا۟ كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْخِزْىِ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَمَتَّعْنَـٰهُمْ إِلَىٰ حِينٍۢ ٩٨
ayahs.translation: Then has there not been a single city that believed so its faith benefited it except the people of Jonah? When they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in worldly life and gave them enjoyment for a time.
ayahs.summary: No town believed to its benefit except the people of Jonah; when they believed We removed their punishment.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Was there any town that believed and its belief benefited it except the people of Jonah? When they believed We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in the worldly life and gave them enjoyment for a time. The people of Jonah (Yūnus) are the exception—they believed and were spared; their story is a sign of the power of collective repentance.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The people of Jonah show that when a people believe, Allah can remove punishment and grant relief—repentance is never in vain.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Those who would not believe until they see the punishment were described; now the exception—the people of Jonah who believed—is given.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: If your Lord had willed, everyone on earth would have believed. Will you then compel people until they are believers?

ayahs.arabic: وَلَوْ شَآءَ رَبُّكَ لَـَٔامَنَ مَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ كُلُّهُمْ جَمِيعًا ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تُكْرِهُ ٱلنَّاسَ حَتَّىٰ يَكُونُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ ٩٩
ayahs.translation: And had your Lord willed, those on earth would have believed—all of them entirely. Then, would you compel the people in order that they become believers?
ayahs.summary: If your Lord had willed, all on earth would believe; you cannot compel belief.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: If your Lord had willed, everyone on earth would have believed, all of them. Will you then compel the people until they are believers? Belief is by Allah's will and human choice; the Prophet cannot and must not compel anyone—guidance is from Allah.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Belief is not compelled; the Prophet's role is to convey, not to force—Allah could have made all believe but has given choice.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The people of Jonah believed and were spared; now the principle that belief cannot be compelled is stated.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: It is not for any soul to believe except by the permission of Allah.

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا كَانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَن تُؤْمِنَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَيَجْعَلُ ٱلرِّجْسَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ ١٠٠
ayahs.translation: And it is not for a soul to believe except by permission of Allāh, and He will place defilement upon those who will not use reason.
ayahs.summary: None can believe except by Allah's leave; He lays defilement on those who do not reason.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: It is not for a soul to believe except by the permission of Allah. And He lays defilement (rijs) upon those who do not use reason. Belief is by Allah's leave; those who refuse to reason are left in defilement.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Belief is a gift from Allah; the one who refuses to use reason is left in spiritual defilement.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet was told he cannot compel belief; now belief is said to be only by Allah's permission.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Say: look at what is in the heavens and the earth.

ayahs.arabic: قُلِ ٱنظُرُوا۟ مَاذَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَمَا تُغْنِى ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتُ وَٱلنُّذُرُ عَن قَوْمٍۢ لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ ١٠١
ayahs.translation: Say, "Observe what is in the heavens and the earth." But of no avail will be signs or warners to a people who do not believe.
ayahs.summary: Say: Look at what is in the heavens and earth; signs and warners do not avail those who do not believe.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Say: look at what is in the heavens and the earth. But signs and warners do not avail a people who do not believe. The creation is there to be observed; for those who refuse to believe, no sign or warner will avail.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The heavens and the earth are full of signs; for those who will not believe, signs and warners are of no avail.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Belief was said to be by Allah's permission; now the addressees are told to look at creation—but signs do not avail the disbelievers.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Do they wait for anything but the like of the days of those who passed before them? Say: wait, I am with you among those who wait.

ayahs.arabic: فَهَلْ يَنتَظِرُونَ إِلَّا مِثْلَ أَيَّامِ ٱلَّذِينَ خَلَوْا۟ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ قُلْ فَٱنتَظِرُوٓا۟ إِنِّى مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُنتَظِرِينَ ١٠٢
ayahs.translation: So do they wait except for like what occurred in the days of those who passed on before them? Say, "Then wait; indeed, I am with you among those who wait."
ayahs.summary: Do they expect anything but the like of what befell former peoples? Wait; I wait with you.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Do they wait for anything but the like of what happened to those who passed away before them? Say: then wait; I am with you among those who wait. The fate of the past is the pattern for the future; the Prophet waits with them for the decree of Allah.
ayahs.lessonReflection: What came upon the past nations can come again; the messenger waits with the people for Allah's decree.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Signs and warners were said not to avail the disbelievers; now they are told to expect the like of the days of the past.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Then We save Our messengers and those who believe; so it is incumbent upon Us to save the believers.

ayahs.arabic: ثُمَّ نُنَجِّى رُسُلَنَا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا نُنجِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ١٠٣
ayahs.translation: Then We will save Our messengers and those who have believed. Thus, it is an obligation upon Us that We save the believers.
ayahs.summary: Then We save Our messengers and those who believed; We are bound to save the believers.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Then We save Our messengers and those who believe. Thus it is incumbent upon Us to save the believers. The promise is explicit: Allah will save the messengers and the believers—it is a duty He has taken upon Himself.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The salvation of the messengers and the believers is a promise and an obligation on Allah—the believers can take heart.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet was told to wait with them; now the promise to save the messengers and the believers is stated.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Say: O people, if you are in doubt about my religion—I do not worship those you worship besides Allah.

ayahs.arabic: قُلْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِن كُنتُمْ فِى شَكٍّۢ مِّن دِينِى فَلَآ أَعْبُدُ ٱلَّذِينَ تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَـٰكِنْ أَعْبُدُ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى يَتَوَفَّىٰكُمْ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ١٠٤
ayahs.translation: Say, "O people, if you are in doubt as to my religion—then I do not worship those which you worship besides Allāh; but I worship Allāh, who causes your death. And I have been commanded to be of the believers.
ayahs.summary: Say: If you doubt my religion, I do not worship what you worship; I worship Allah who takes your souls.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Say: O people, if you are in doubt about my religion—I do not worship those you worship besides Allah, but I worship Allah who takes your souls, and I am commanded to be among the believers. The Prophet's worship is for Allah alone, who holds life and death; he is commanded to believe.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The Prophet's religion is clear: worship of Allah alone, who takes the souls, and obedience to the command to believe.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The salvation of the messengers and believers was promised; now the Prophet is given a clear declaration of his religion.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Set your face to the religion as a ḥanīf and do not be among the associators.

ayahs.arabic: وَأَنْ أَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًۭا وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ ١٠٥
ayahs.translation: And commanded, 'Direct your face toward the religion, inclining to truth, and never be of those who associate others with Allāh;
ayahs.summary: Set your face to the religion in sincerity and do not be among the associators.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: And: set your face to the religion as a ḥanīf (inclining to truth), and do not be among the associators. The command is to direct the face—the whole orientation—to the religion in sincerity and to avoid shirk.
ayahs.lessonReflection: To set one's face to the religion as a ḥanīf is to worship Allah alone in sincerity and to shun association.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet declared he worships Allah who takes the souls; now he is commanded to set his face to the religion as a ḥanīf.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Do not call upon besides Allah what neither benefits you nor harms you.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تَدْعُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُكَ وَلَا يَضُرُّكَ ۖ فَإِن فَعَلْتَ فَإِنَّكَ إِذًۭا مِّنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ ١٠٦
ayahs.translation: And do not invoke besides Allāh that which neither benefits you nor harms you, for if you did, then indeed you would be of the wrongdoers.'"
ayahs.summary: Do not call on besides Allah what neither benefits nor harms you, or you will be wrongdoers.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: And do not call upon besides Allah what neither benefits you nor harms you; for if you did you would then be among the wrongdoers. To invoke what has no power over benefit or harm is futile and wrong; the wrongdoer is the one who associates such with Allah.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Invoking what can neither benefit nor harm is futile and places one among the wrongdoers.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet was commanded to set his face to the religion as a ḥanīf; now he is told not to invoke others besides Allah.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: If Allah touches you with harm, none can remove it but He; if He desires good for you, none can repel His bounty.

ayahs.arabic: وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ ٱللَّهُ بِضُرٍّۢ فَلَا كَاشِفَ لَهُۥٓ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ وَإِن يُرِدْكَ بِخَيْرٍۢ فَلَا رَآدَّ لِفَضْلِهِۦ ۚ يُصِيبُ بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ١٠٧
ayahs.translation: And if Allāh should touch you with adversity, there is no remover of it except Him; and if He intends for you good, then there is no repeller of His bounty. He causes it to reach whom He wills of His servants. And He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.
ayahs.summary: If Allah afflicts you, none can remove it but He; if He wills good, none can repel His bounty; He is Forgiving, Merciful.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: If Allah touches you with harm, none can remove it but He; and if He desires good for you, none can repel His bounty. He gives it to whom He will of His servants. He is the Forgiving, the Merciful. All harm and good are in His hand; He is the only one who can remove harm or bestow good; He is Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Harm and good are with Allah alone; to turn to Him for relief and for bounty is to turn to the only one who can give them.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet was told not to invoke what neither benefits nor harms; now harm and good are said to be only from Allah.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Say: O people, the truth has come to you from your Lord; whoever is guided is guided for his soul, whoever goes astray goes astray against it.

ayahs.arabic: قُلْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ قَدْ جَآءَكُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَهْتَدِى لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن ضَلَّ فَإِنَّمَا يَضِلُّ عَلَيْهَا ۖ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ عَلَيْكُم بِوَكِيلٍۢ ١٠٨
ayahs.translation: Say, "O mankind, the truth has come to you from your Lord, so whoever is guided is only guided for the benefit of his soul, and whoever goes astray only goes astray against it. And I am not over you a manager."
ayahs.summary: The truth has come from your Lord; whoever is guided is guided for himself, whoever goes astray does so against himself; I am not your keeper.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Say: O people, the truth has come to you from your Lord. Whoever is guided is guided for his own soul, and whoever goes astray goes astray against it. I am not a guardian over you. The message is delivered; guidance and misguidance are for the soul's own account; the Prophet is not responsible for their choice.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The truth has come; each soul answers for its own guidance or misguidance; the messenger is not their keeper.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Allah was said to be the only one who removes harm and gives bounty; now the Prophet is given the final address to the people.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Follow what is revealed to you and be patient until Allah judges; He is the best of judges.

ayahs.arabic: وَٱتَّبِعْ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ وَٱصْبِرْ حَتَّىٰ يَحْكُمَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلْحَـٰكِمِينَ ١٠٩
ayahs.translation: And follow what is revealed to you, and be patient until Allāh will judge. And He is the best of judges.
ayahs.summary: Follow what is revealed to you and be patient until Allah judges; He is the best of judges.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Follow what is revealed to you and be patient until Allah judges. He is the best of judges. The closing command: adhere to the revelation and be patient; the judgment belongs to Allah and He is the best of judges. The surah ends with the same themes with which it began—revelation, patience, and the return to Allah.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The messenger is to follow the revelation and be patient; the judgment is with Allah, the best of judges.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The truth was said to have come and each soul to be accountable for its own guidance; now the Prophet is commanded to follow the revelation and be patient.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: The surah ends with the command to follow revelation and be patient until Allah judges.
